iterations.theme: Global Financial Stability, Banking System Resilience, and Policy Responses in a Post-Inflationary Environment
iterations.reason_for_cut: The entire transcript is less than the minimum length (35 minutes) for a single iteration, thus it forms one complete thematic block covering the panel discussion.

chapters.topic: Introduction: Panel Questions on Banking System Stability
Analysis of Recent Banking Failures: Idiosyncratic vs. Systemic Risks
Limited Nature of Current Vulnerabilities and Role of Supervision
Banking System Health: Comparison of US/Eurozone with China/Japan
Factors Contributing to Banking System Resilience and Risk Control
Emerging Risks: Non-Bank Financial Sector and Liquidity Concerns
Potential for Soft Landing: Resilient Labor Markets and Private Balance Sheets
Managing 'Small Surprises' and System Adaptation to Stress
Appropriate Policy Responses: Monetary Policy vs. Liquidity Provision
Speaker 2: Focus on US Banking Situation and Supervisory Failures
Detailed Analysis of US Banking Failures (SVB, Signature) and Supervisory Lapses
Consequences of Systemic Supervisory Failure and Crisis Management
Systemic Risk Exception and Unlimited Deposit Guarantees in the US
Debate on the True Nature of Systemic Risk in Recent Events
European Banking Supervision: Absence of Comparable Weakness
Historical Precedents for Deposit Guarantees (Ireland vs. Iceland)
Speaker 3: Bank Group Strategy and Regional Stress Test Experiences
Practical Resilience: Surviving High Interest Rate Shocks in CEE
Concerns: The Cost of Disinflation and Potential Asset Devaluation
Speaker 4: Concluding Remarks on the Scale of Recent Banking Shocks
